State of CaJifomia 
Memorandum 
To 
Commissioners Gupta, Houston, 
McAndrews, Metzger and Wade 
From 
: 
FAIR POUTICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Subject: Review of Commission Regulations 
Date 
i"lay 21, 1981 
/-
/ / 
Recent amendments to the Administrative Procedure Act require 
each agency to review its regulations during 1981. 
At the May 
meeting, the first group of regulations was presented to you for 
your review. 
At the June meeting, the second installment, concerning 
lobbyists, is presented to you. 
As with the last month's review, the staff's procedures were 
to review our advice letter file and interview staff members to 
determine what regulations had been a source of confusion or diffi-
culty in recent years. 
We also examined recent changes in the Act 
and court decisions to determine whether they necessitated any 
changes in our regulations. 
We tried to avoid the temptation to 
make merely stylistic changes; however, errors have been corrected. 
Once these steps were completed, we translated our findings 
into proposed changes in the Commission's regulations. 
These changes 
generally may be broken down into two categories: 
Substantive--
changes involving the discretionary policy of the Commission to 
interpret the Act; and technical -- changes made necessary by recent 
amendments to the Act and court decisions, to correct minor incon-
sistencies among regulations, 
or to correct grammatical or punctuation 
errors. 
In addition to the notice required by the Administrative ?rocedure 
Act, we noticed the proposed changes in the Lobbyist Bulletin, 
dated May 13, 1981. 
Following is a summary of all the regulations reviewed, indicating 
whether the proposed changes to each regulation are essentially 
substantive or technical. 
The accompanying Statement of Reasons 
will explain the proposed changes in more detail. 


Commissioners Gupta, Houston, 
McAndrews, Metzger and Wade 
May 21, 1981 
Page Two 
Substantive 
Change 
Technical 
Change 
No Change 
18616 
18623 
18239 
18600 
18601 
18619 
18650 
18202 
18615 
18618 
18620 
18622 
18630 
To date, I have had several calls and one letter reyarding the 
proposed amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18616, which would 
require lobbyists to indicate, on their reports, the 5 or 10 bills 
on which they have spent the most time during each reporting period. 
Thus far, all the comments and questions have come exclusively from 
"association" lobbyists, as opposed to "contract" lobbyists. 
An 
"association" lobbyist is one employed full-time as a lobbyist by 
one employer. 
"Contract lobbyists" represent numerous employers. 
Specifically, calls have been received from the following: 
Jim Evans, United Transportation Union; 
Peter Schilla, Western Center on Law & Poverty; 
Don Reisner, California Railroad Association; 
Jim Eller, California Farm Bureau Federation; 
Jack Schreder, San Diego City School District; 
Dick Kelley, California Forest Protective Association; 
Emery Dowell, Blue Cross Plans of California (Letter); 
Bill Thomas, California Food Producers, Inc. 
Only 3 lobbyists have made specific comments, another has written a 
general letter. 
The comments all have expressed concern, reservation, 
or displeasure with the new requirement. 
One lobbyist stated that 
he currently keeps no time records, so the requirement would be 
burdensome to him. 
He now reports his full salary, and does not 
have to keep track of how much time he spends on each bill, since 
he is full-time lobbyist for only one association. 
I asked him his 
opinion of the possible alternative of using the standard of "most 
important" as opposed to "most time." 
He felt this was too sub-
jective and also was concerned that his employers might be upset if 
his determination of "most important" did not coincide with theirs. 
Two other lobbyists suggested as an alternative to our proposal 
that a de minimus test be devised to eliminate over-reporting of 
minor bills on which little or nothing is done. 
For example elimi-
nate reporting when a lobbyist only contacts the author's office, 


Commissioners, Gupta, Houston, 
McAndrews, Metzger and Wade 
May 21, 1981 
Page Three 
the author, or the committee consultant to find out what a bill 
really does, and then takes no further action. 
Now lobbyists report 
this, but if we instituted a de minimus test, they wouldn't have to 
and would only report their major bills. 
The problem with this 
suggestion is developing a workable de minimus standard. 
One phone 
call from some lobbyists may be sufficient to kill a bill, for 
others, much greater effort is involved. 
Furthermore, there now 
exists a form of de minimus test since the Commission only requires 
that lobbyists identify "specific legislation ••• he or she has 
actively influenced Qr attempted to influence during the reporting 
period." 
FPPC Lobbyist Manual, page 14. 
Enclosed in your packet 
is a copy of the only letter received to date, from Mr. Dowell, 
which underscores the problem of over-reporting. 
When he reports 
109 bills, the public won't know what bills he is actively or ex-
tensively working. 
Another lobbyist raised the following concern with the "most 
time spent" standard. 
He may spend 3 solid days sitting in Ways & 
Means Committee waiting for 20 bills that he is following to be 
heard. 
How does he attribute that time among these 20 bills? 
What 
if he doesn't have to testify on 4 of the bill~, but does on the 
other 16? 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 


SRu95lJ 
. 
. <-OOF OF PUBLICATION 
(2015.S CCP) 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
S.S. 
County of Sacramento 
I am the principal clerk of THE DAILY RECORDER, a 
newspaper of general circulation published in the City of 
Sacramento. County of Sacramento, and whi<:h newspaper 
has been adjudged a newspaper of general circulation by the 
Superior Court of the County of Sacramento. State of Califor-
nia, under date of May 2, 1913, Case Number 16,180 that the 
notice. of which the annexed is a printed copy (set in type not 
smaller than non-pareil) has been published in each regular 
and entire issue of said newspaper and not in any supplement 
thereof on the following dates, to wit: 
4/17 
I certify (or declare) under penalty of perjury that the 
foregoing is true and correct. 
Date 
r--; 
--' 
::: 
s::: 
SIgnature 
~ 
Apr. 17, 1981 
THE' DAILY RECORDER 
"Serving Sacramento Since 1911" 
1115 "H" Street, P.O. Box 1048 
Sacramento, CA 95805 
(916) 444-2355 


State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
: Office of Administrative Law 
Date 
July 24, 1981 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Subject: Statement of Review Completion 
The Fair Political Practices Commission has completed its 
review pursuant to Government Code Sections 11349, et ~. of 
Chapters 2 and 6 of Title 2 of the California Administrative Code 
the following regulations were included in this review: 
Regula tion 
18202 
18239 
18600 
18601 
18615 
18616 
18618 
18619 
18620 
18622 
18623 
18630 
18650 
Disposition 
No Change 
Amended 
No Change 
Amended 
No Chan<;;le 
No Change 
No Change 
Amenaed 
No Change 
No Change 
Amended 
No Change 
Repealed 
Pursuant to Government Code Section 11347.3, the following 
are attached to this memo: 
A. 
Notices of Proposed Review of Regulations. 
B. 
Original Statements of Reasons and proposals for 
changes in the regulations. 
C. 
Written comments submitted on the regulations. 
(The 
written comment consists of several letters; some 
public testimony was given on Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. 
Code Section 18616 only.) 


Office of Administrative Law 
July 24, 1981 
Page Two 
D. 
Minutes of the public hearings held by the COlrunission 
on the reyulations. 
E. 
Final Statements of Reasons and rhe regulations as 
finally acted upon by the Commission. 
F. 
Tapes of the Commission's public hearings on the 
regulations. 
Note: 
This packet includes amended and repealed regulations 
which were a part of the review process. 
For your convenience, 
the regulations and final Statements of Reasons are organized in 
the same order and by the same categories as they are listed on 
the Face Sheet, next attached. 
Also, those reyulations, for 
which there was no change made in the review process, still 
retain the authority and reference lines at the top. 
Your editor 
will change these to the new appropriate location. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 


"Unapproved and Subject to Correction" 
MINUTES OF MEETING 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
June 1, 1981 
Sacramento, California 
1. 
The meeting of the Fair Political Practices Commission convened 
at '10:02 a.m. on June 1, 1981, in Room 150 of the State Personnel 
Board, 801 Capitol Mall, Sacramento, California. 
The chairman 
called the meeting to order. 
All members of the Commission 
were present. 
2. 
A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Mr. Metzger 
to dispense with a reading of the minutes of the May 4, 1981, 
meeting and approve the minutes as distributed. 
AYES: Gupta, 
McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
3. 
The Commission considered the proposed amendment to 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Section 18616 -
to require lobbyists to designate 
by use of an asterisk, those bills on which they have spent 
the most time in each reporting period. 
Robert Leidigh made 
the staff presentation. 
Public testimony was heard from: 
Robert Monagan, California Manufacturers Association" 
Allan E. Tebbetts, attorney for IGA; Suzanne Hutchinson, 
California Chamber of Commerce; and, Don Collin, a registered 
lobbyist. 
A motion was made by Mr. Wade and seconded by 
Ms. McAndrews to reject this proposed amendment. 
AYES: Gupta, 
McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
The staff was then instructed to research past lobbyist reports 
to ascertain how many bills are reported by the "average" 
lobbyist. 
4. 
The Commission considered the following regulations: 
a. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 -
amendment to correct a 
typographical error which misplaced the term "or adminis-
trative." 
Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was presented. 
A motion was made by 
Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the 
amendment. 
AYES: Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and 
Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
b. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18600 -
amendment to correct a 
typographical error and to correct a term so as to be 
consistent with statutory definition. 
Robert Leidigh 
made the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was 


MINUTES OF MEETING 
Page Two 
presented. 
A motion was made by Ms. McAndrews and 
seconded by Mr. Houston that the amendment no~ be approved. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
c. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18601 - to delete reference to 
Section 86202. 
Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was presented. 
A motion was made by 
MI. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the 
amendment. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and 
Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
d. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18619 -
to delete reference to 
Section 86202. 
Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was presented. 
A motion was made by 
Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Wade to approve the amend-
mend. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
e. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18623 - to delete "exchanges" 
from subdivision (b), make conforming changes to the 
regulation and to clarify to whom certain other provisions 
apply. 
Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation. 
No 
public testimony was offered. 
A motion was made by 
Houston and seconded by Ms. Gupta to approve the amendment. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
f. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section l86S0 - repeal the regulation. 
Robert Leidigh made the staff presentation. 
No public 
testimony was presented. 
A motion was made by Mr. Houston 
and seconded by Ms. McAndrews that the regulation be repealed. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
g. 
A motion was made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Metzger 
to approve the follpwing regulations with no change: 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18202, l861S, 18618, 18620, 18622 
and 18630. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and 
Houston. 
Th~ motion was carried. 
S. 
The Commission considered a memorandum from Robert Stern 
regarding possible statutory limitation on lobbyist contri-
butions. 
Barbara Milman made the staff presentation. 
No 
public testimony was presented. 
The Commission instructed the 
staff to insure wider d~stribution of the proposal so that 
the Commission could receive more public input. 
Further dis-
cussion of the proposed legislative amendment was put off. 
6. 
The Commission discussed a memorandum from Barbara Milman 
regarding advising lobbyists that "gifts" made to elected 
officials in the form of picking up dinner tabs, etc. could 
not be treated or reported as contributions. 
Barbara Milman 
made the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was offered. 


MINUTES OF MEETING 
Page Three 
A motion was made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Mr. Wade 
that a short article be published in the Lobbyist·Bulletin 
providing such advice. 
AYES: 
Gupta, r·1cAndrews, Metzger, 
Wade and Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
7. 
The Commission adjourned for lunch at 11:50 a.m. to reconvene 
in Executive Session at 1:15 p.m., the public session to 
reconvene at 1:45 p.m. 
8. 
The Commission considered 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18430. 
Barbara Milman made the staff presentation. 
Public testimony 
was heard from John Hodgson, Dobbs & Nielsen. 
A motion was 
made by Mr. Houston and seconded by Ms. Gupta to set up a 
task force of outside experts to assist the staff in formulating 
a new draft regulation and accompanying draft reporting forms. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
9. 
The Commission considered the following regulations: 
a. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18306 -
compensation and reim-
bursement for members of the FPPC. 
Barbara Milman made 
the staff presentation. 
No public testimony was offered. 
A motion was made by Ms. McAndrews and seconded by Mr. Metzger 
to amend subsection (a) (2) redefining "necessary travel. R 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger and Wade. 
ABSTAIN: Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
A motion was made by Mr. Houston 
and seconded by Ms. Gupta to amend subsection (b) (2) 
deleting Rwhile traveling" for reimbursement of telephone 
expenses and to adopt the regulation as so amended. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
b. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18322 -
procedures for hearings 
on opinion requests. 
Barbara Milman made the staff presenta-
tion. 
No public testimony was offered. 
A motion was made 
by Mr. Houston and seconded by Ms. McAndrews to remove the 
5 day time limits for submitting written materials to the 
Commission in subsections (b) and (d). 
AYES: McAndrews 
and Houston. 
NOES: 
Gupta, Metzger and Wade. 
The motion 
was defeated. 
A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded 
by Mr. Houston to delete the last sentence in subsection (d) 
and to add subsection (e) stating that all time limits 
imposed by the regulation might be waived by the Commission, 
and to adopt the regulation as amended. 
AYES: 
Gupta, 
Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
NOES: 
McAndrews. 
The motion 
was carried. 


MINUTES OF MEETING 
Page Four 
c. 
A motion was made by Ms. Mc~~drews and seconded by 
Ms. Gupta to approve all housekeeping regulations as pre-
sented by staff and to amend 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18351 to add the position of Special Assistant to the 
Commission to the list of "designated positions" and 
disclosure categories of the FPPC's Conflict of Interest 
Code. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The motion was carried. 
10. 
A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Ms. McAndrews to 
approve the Conflict of Interest Codes and amendments to 
Conflict of Interest Codes as. presented by the staff. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
The following were approved: 
Conflict of Interest Codes 
Office of Administrative Law 
Metropolitan Transportation Commission 
North Kern Vocational Training Center 
Central Coast Cities Self-Insurance Fund 
Amendments to Conflict of Interest Codes 
Serra Cooperative Library System 
Department of Motor Vehicles 
11. 
The Commission considered a memorandum from. Jay Greenwood 
regarding a proposed study of campaign contributions received 
by legislative candidates. 
A motion was made by Mr. Houston 
and seconded by Mr. Metzger to approve the proposed study. 
AYES: 
Gupta, Metzger and Houston. 
NOES: 
McAndrews and Wade. 
The motion was carried. 
12. 
A motion was made by Ms. Gupta and seconded by Mr. Houston 
to adjourn the public session and meet in executive session. 
AYES: 
Gupta, McAndrews, Metzger, Wade and Houston. 
The 
motion was carried. 
13. 
The meeting was adjourned at 4:33 p.m.

LOB B Y 1ST B U L LET I N 
FPPC TO REVIEW AND AMEND 
LOBBYIST REGULATIONS 
~nts 
of Economic Int .... 1It 
322-60U4 
VOL. 5J No.1 
May 13, 1981 
The FPPC, along with all other state agencies, is undergoing a 
complete review of its regulations (Cal. Adm. Code, Titie i~ 
Division 6). 
Those dealing with lobbying will be considered by 
the Commission at its June 1st meeting. 
All lobbyist regulations 
are proposed to be continued without change exce9t those which 
follow. 
.~ost of these changes are non-substantive and merely 
reflect changes in the law or corrections of typographical errors. 
The proposed change 
most likely to be of interest to lobbyists 
is found in Section 18616. 
Written and oral comments are welcomed. 
For further information concerning these proposed changes, contact 
Robert E. Leidigh, Counsel, FPPC Legal Division, (916)322-5901. 
Amend Section 18239 -
To correct a typographical error which 
misplaced the term "or administrative" in subdivision (e) (2) (B)l. 
rather than in subdivision (e) (2) {A)l. where it belongs. 
(Government Code Sections 82002, 82004, 82032, 82039) 
Amend Section 18600 -
To correct a term so as to be consistent 
with statutory definition. 
(Government Code Sections 82021, 
86100-86111, 86200-86205) 
Amend Section 18601 -
To delete reference to Section 86202, 
which was invalidated by the California Supreme Court, FPPC v. 
Superior Court, 25 Cal. 3d 33 (1979). 
(Government Code Sections 
86100-86103, 86202) 
Amend Section 18616 -
To require lobbyists to designate, by use 
of an asterisk, those bills on which they have spent the most 
time in each reporting period. 
(Government Code Sections 86105, 
86107) 
(over)

sections.question_synthetic: This document is not an advice letter seeking legal analysis but rather internal FPPC memoranda and meeting minutes regarding regulatory review and amendment processes under the Administrative Procedure Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a compilation of internal FPPC memoranda, meeting minutes, and regulatory materials rather than an advice letter. No traditional Q/A sections present. Contains May 21, 1981 memo to Commissioners on regulation review, proof of publication, July 24, 1981 memo to Office of Administrative Law, and June 1, 1981 meeting minutes. Primary focus is regulatory amendment process, not legal advice to external parties.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: This document is not an advice letter seeking legal analysis but rather internal FPPC memoranda and meeting minutes regarding regulatory review and amendment processes under the Administrative Procedure Act.
embedding.first_500_words: State of CaJifomia Memorandum To Commissioners Gupta, Houston, McAndrews, Metzger and Wade From : FAIR POUTICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Robert E. Leidigh Subject: Review of Commission Regulations Date i"lay 21, 1981 /- / / Recent amendments to the Administrative Procedure Act require each agency to review its regulations during 1981. At the May meeting, the first group of regulations was presented to you for your review. At the June meeting, the second installment, concerning lobbyists, is presented to you. As with the last month's review, the staff's procedures were to review our advice letter file and interview staff members to determine what regulations had been a source of confusion or diffi- culty in recent years. We also examined recent changes in the Act and court decisions to determine whether they necessitated any changes in our regulations. We tried to avoid the temptation to make merely stylistic changes; however, errors have been corrected. Once these steps were completed, we translated our findings into proposed changes in the Commission's regulations. These changes generally may be broken down into two categories: Substantive-- changes involving the discretionary policy of the Commission to interpret the Act; and technical -- changes made necessary by recent amendments to the Act and court decisions, to correct minor incon- sistencies among regulations, or to correct grammatical or punctuation errors. In addition to the notice required by the Administrative ?rocedure Act, we noticed the proposed changes in the Lobbyist Bulletin, dated May 13, 1981. Following is a summary of all the regulations reviewed, indicating whether the proposed changes to each regulation are essentially substantive or technical. The accompanying Statement of Reasons will explain the proposed changes in more detail. Commissioners Gupta, Houston, McAndrews, Metzger and Wade May 21, 1981 Page Two Substantive Change Technical Change No Change 18616 18623 18239 18600 18601 18619 18650 18202 18615 18618 18620 18622 18630 To date, I have had several calls and one letter reyarding the proposed amendment to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18616, which would require lobbyists to indicate, on their reports, the 5 or 10 bills on which they have spent the most time during each reporting period. Thus far, all the comments and questions have come exclusively from "association" lobbyists, as opposed to "contract" lobbyists. An "association" lobbyist is one employed full-time as a lobbyist by one employer. "Contract lobbyists" represent numerous employers. Specifically, calls have been received from the following: Jim Evans, United Transportation Union; Peter Schilla, Western Center on Law & Poverty; Don Reisner, California Railroad Association; Jim Eller, California Farm Bureau Federation; Jack Schreder, San Diego City School District; Dick Kelley, California Forest Protective Association; Emery Dowell, Blue Cross Plans of California (Letter); Bill Thomas, California Food Producers, Inc. Only 3 lobbyists have made specific comments, another has written a general letter. The comments all have expressed concern, reservation, or displeasure with the new requirement. One lobbyist stated that he currently keeps no time records, so the requirement would be burdensome to him. He now reports his full
embedding.summary: This document collection contains FPPC internal memoranda from May-July 1981 regarding the Commission's regulatory review process, including staff presentations on proposed amendments to lobbyist reporting regulations, public testimony, and Commission meeting minutes documenting votes on regulatory changes.
